summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js exfiltrates ~/.ssh and environment variables to Telegram on import
details: Package name `@solana-labs/etherjs` impersonates the official `@solana/web3.js` (legitimate maintainer scope is `@solana`). The library entry point `lib/index.cjs.js` (and the parallel `lib/index.esm.js`) appends a credential-harvesting payload to an otherwise copied web3.js codebase. On `require()`/`import` of the package, the payload uses `child_process`, `http`/`https`, `os.homedir()`, and reads of `~/.ssh` and `process.env` to gather the installer's SSH directory contents and environment variables, then POSTs them to `https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage`. The Telegram bot token (`8628389567:AAHeoLi034Vg6JIXsC_vqP-v-PXH2FhZIG4`) and chat id (`8346336575`) are base64-embedded at lib/index.cjs.js:11416 and:11418 to evade scanners and reconstructed at runtime. The same harvester block is duplicated in the ESM bundle (lines ~11310, 11354, 11448, 11515, 11541, 11571) and referenced in the IIFE bundles, so any consumer entry point reaches it. Trigger is on-import: `main` is `lib/index.cjs.js` and the payload runs at module top level.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
